skill.name: exploration-creator
skill.description: Create structured exploration content for the SHINOBI WAY game, including regions, locations, rooms, intel missions, and path networks.
skill.summary: Build node-based game exploration content with validated regions, rooms, intel missions, and path choices.
skill.author: CesarAugustusGroB

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found they are false positives from Markdown code fences, TypeScript examples, diagrams, and game design terminology. No prompt injection, secret access, network calls, executable scripts, or malicious intent were found in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command detections are Markdown fences and inline TypeScript examples. They document game data structures and diagrams, not executable shell commands or Ruby backtick execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are fenced Markdown blocks containing hierarchy diagrams or TypeScript declarations. I found no shell command syntax, subprocess API, or instruction to execute these examples.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Game Terms Flagged as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography detections match ordinary words and TypeScript identifiers such as description, destination, and ElementType. No hashing, encryption, signing, or random token generation API is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines contain prose or game model fields, not cryptographic code. The broader file review did not show MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or similar algorithm usage.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Validation Checklists Flagged as Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance detections are game-content validation checklist items about reachable locations, valid paths, and mission definitions. They do not inspect the host, network, files, users, or processes.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited text is a design checklist for game maps. There is no command, API call, or procedure for host or network reconnaissance.

content.value_statement: Designing branching exploration content can become inconsistent across regions, rooms, paths, and rewards. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a clear structure for creating validated SHINOBI WAY exploration content.
actual_capabilities: Defines the Region to Location to Room hierarchy for exploration content.
Creates 10 to 15 location region plans with entry points, paths, secrets, and a boss destination.
Specifies 10-room branching layouts with fixed room connections and activity weights.
Designs intel missions that control path choice, random travel, loop discovery, and rewards.
Provides TypeScript interface references and templates for game integration.
Includes a complete Land of Waves example for structure and validation.
limitations: It targets the SHINOBI WAY exploration system and may not fit unrelated games without adaptation.
It generates design structures and TypeScript-style content, not a complete playable build.
It does not verify balance through simulation or automated gameplay testing.
It assumes the host project already has compatible enemy, loot, event, and story systems.

use_cases.title: Build a New Region
use_cases.description: Create a complete exploration region with locations, danger progression, secret routes, and a final boss path.

use_cases.title: Plan Branching Room Content
use_cases.description: Map room activities, intel missions, story events, and player choices for a location.

use_cases.title: Prepare Integration Specs
use_cases.description: Convert exploration concepts into TypeScript-ready structures that match the documented interfaces.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Simple Region
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new SHINOBI WAY exploration region with 10 locations, two entry points, one boss location, and a clear danger curve.

prompt_templates.title: Design Location Rooms
prompt_templates.prompt: Design the 10-room layout for a wilderness location, including activity weights, room 10 intel mission, and validation notes.

prompt_templates.title: Map Paths and Secrets
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a path network for this region with forward paths, branches, one-time loops, secret unlocks, and intel rewards.

prompt_templates.title: Produce Integration Content
prompt_templates.prompt: Turn this region concept into TypeScript-ready exploration content that follows the Region, Location, Room, IntelMission, and Path structures.

output_examples.input: Create a misty mountain pass region with hidden shrine routes.
output_examples.output: A complete region plan with entry cliffs, mid-tier passes, secret shrine access, loop paths, elite intel encounters, and a boss summit.

output_examples.input: Design rooms for a fortified checkpoint location.
output_examples.output: A 10-room progression with combat-heavy activity weights, one merchant restriction, room connections, and an elite guard intel mission.

output_examples.input: Add meaningful path choices after an intel mission.
output_examples.output: A path set that reveals safe, dangerous, secret, and loop options with destination hints and unlock requirements.

best_practices: Start with the region danger curve before writing individual locations.
Define intel rewards before finalizing path choices and secret routes.
Run the included validation checklists after each region, location, and room draft.
anti_patterns: Do not create disconnected locations that cannot be reached from an entry point.
Do not make room 10 random; it must be an intel mission or boss encounter.
Do not add too many loops or secret paths without clear unlock conditions.

faq.question: What game system is this skill designed for?
faq.answer: It is designed for SHINOBI WAY: THE INFINITE TOWER and its node-based exploration model.

faq.question: Can it create complete regions?
faq.answer: Yes. It guides creation of regions, locations, room layouts, intel missions, paths, loops, secrets, and validation checks.

faq.question: Does it write production TypeScript?
faq.answer: It provides TypeScript-ready structures and examples, but developers should still integrate and test them in the game codebase.

faq.question: Does it support random room generation?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents activity weights for rooms 1 through 9 while keeping room 10 fixed as an intel mission.

faq.question: Can players backtrack in these regions?
faq.answer: Normal travel is forward-only. Special one-time loop paths can return to earlier visited locations when discovered.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: The audit found no malicious behavior. Static alerts were false positives from Markdown examples and game terminology.
